tasks.title: Lock Referral Source Vocabulary
tasks.verification_requirement: Exactly one surviving non-purchase membership source label is chosen; the label is `referral`; `invite` is retired from target vocabulary.
stop_conditions: Any proposal preserves multiple surviving labels
Any proposal keeps `invite` as canonical vocabulary

tasks.title: Lock Canonical Create-Profile Surface And Ownership Split
tasks.verification_requirement: Canonical create-profile execution surface is `POST /auth/onboarding/create-profile`; `/profiles/me` remains projection-only; required name, optional bio, and optional image ownership split is explicit.
stop_conditions: Any proposal makes `/profiles/me` create-profile authority
Any proposal makes create-profile media authority

tasks.title: Lock Canonical Application-Subject Naming
tasks.verification_requirement: `app.auth_subjects` is named as the canonical application subject authority for onboarding subject state, app-level role subject fields, and app-level admin subject fields.
stop_conditions: Any competing subject-owner naming survives
Any proposal weakens the canonical authority phrase

tasks.title: Rewrite Active Contract Corpus To Remove Invite As Active Doctrine
tasks.verification_requirement: Invite no longer survives as active canonical doctrine in rewritten active contracts; referral is the sole canonical non-purchase grant doctrine in the rewritten active contracts.
stop_conditions: Any rewritten active contract keeps invite as active doctrine
Any rewritten active contract reopens T01, T02, or T03

tasks.title: Append-Only Baseline Referral Vocabulary Alignment
tasks.verification_requirement: A new append-only slot, not an in-place edit, aligns membership-source vocabulary and comments from `invite` to `referral`.
stop_conditions: Any proposal edits slot `0032` in place
Any proposal mutates unrelated purchase/payment or course-access doctrine

tasks.title: Remove Duplicate Backend App Entry Model
tasks.verification_requirement: `GET /entry-state` remains the sole post-auth routing authority and backend guards enforce without defining a second app-entry model.
stop_conditions: Any backend guard continues to derive separate entry semantics
Any change makes `/profiles/me` or token claims entry authority

tasks.title: Replace Tests That Canonize require_app_entry
mutation_scope: frontend tests that assert obsolete guard doctrine
tasks.verification_requirement: Tests assert `GET /entry-state` as sole routing authority and treat backend guards as enforcement reuse only.
stop_conditions: Any replacement test still canonizes `require_app_entry` as authority
Any test encodes a second app-entry model

tasks.title: Remove Profile-Derived Onboarding Completion
tasks.verification_requirement: Onboarding completion depends only on explicit completion semantics and `app.auth_subjects.onboarding_state`, not profile-name presence.
stop_conditions: Any implementation still blocks completion on `display_name`
Any fallback turns profile projection into onboarding authority

tasks.title: Move Required Name To Create-Profile
mutation_scope: backend auth register schemas and routes
tasks.verification_requirement: Registration no longer requires name and the required name is enforced only at canonical create-profile.
stop_conditions: Any path keeps required name on registration
Any path moves required name away from create-profile

tasks.title: Separate Create-Profile From /profiles/me
tasks.verification_requirement: `POST /auth/onboarding/create-profile` becomes the onboarding-owned mutation surface and `/profiles/me` remains projection-only.
stop_conditions: Any implementation routes create-profile through `/profiles/me`
Any implementation moves media authority into create-profile

tasks.title: Rewire Referral Transport And Membership Handoff
tasks.verification_requirement: Referral email transport lands at create-profile and referral-derived membership handoff uses source `referral` without creating purchase/payment truth.
stop_conditions: Any runtime handoff still uses source `invite`
Any referral transport lands outside canonical create-profile onboarding

tasks.title: Remove Active Invite Surfaces
mutation_scope: derived docs that still present invite as active runtime doctrine
tasks.verification_requirement: Invite no longer survives as an active runtime, frontend, fixture, or test surface and referral remains the only canonical non-purchase grant doctrine for this onboarding path.
stop_conditions: Any active invite token or invite membership path survives as runtime doctrine
Any removal breaks the locked referral grant path
